description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two prominent black metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood displays a natural grain pattern with visible knots and subtle color variations, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat, composed of the same wood as the sides, and the top is fully enclosed, indicating the barrel is sealed. There is a small, round wooden plug or bung on one side, typically used for filling or emptying the barrel. The metal hoops are matte and slightly textured, providing structural reinforcement and a visual contrast to the wood. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns, where it may be used for storage or decorative purposes.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with black metal hoops, sealed top, and visible wood grain, featuring a small side bung.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden staves, black metal hoops, and a visible side bung.
description_view_1: Side view with uninterrupted wood grain and both metal hoops clearly visible, no bung present.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with wood grain and knots, both hoops visible, no bung present.
description_view_3: Rear view with wood grain and knots, both hoops visible, no bung present.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: black metal hoops at top and bottom
